sources.name: Utility tariff filings and rate sheets
sources.role: Primary itemized source for fixed charges, delivery/distribution, transmission, riders, and taxes. Captured directly from each utility's published residential tariff PDF or rate sheet.

sources.name: OpenEI U.S. Utility Rate Database (URDB)
sources.role: Independent bundled all-in cross-check, modeled at 900 kWh. Used to validate the all-in cents/kWh figure for each utility and to provide the all-in number where a full tariff breakdown was not available.

sources.name: U.S. Energy Information Administration (EIA)
sources.role: State residential average price reference. Each modeled all-in rate is reconciled against the EIA state average so the figure stays within a defensible band.

nonEnergyDefinition: Everything on a residential electricity bill except the supply (generation) energy commodity rate: the fixed customer charge, delivery/distribution, transmission, riders (efficiency, renewable, storm, wildfire, recovery, etc.), and taxes/franchise fees. One-time fees and minimum-bill floors are excluded from the recurring monthly add-on.

confidenceDefinitions.high: The all-in rate is corroborated by both the utility tariff and OpenEI URDB, with both within +/-20% of the EIA state average.
confidenceDefinitions.medium: The all-in rate comes from a single validated source (either the utility tariff or URDB) that reconciles with the EIA state average.
confidenceDefinitions.low: An individual line item flagged during QA (for example a stale effective date or a value outside the expected sanity band). Low-confidence line items are documented but excluded from headline totals where noted.

coverageNote: Coverage is intentionally honest about its limits: 3 states have a complete itemized non-energy breakdown (California, Florida, Nevada), Texas is shown as delivery-only because it is deregulated, and 26 states have a single validated all-in rate. Delaware, Oregon, and Wisconsin were pulled from the itemized breakdowns pending a fix to their energy-versus-delivery split, though their all-in rates remain in the table. This set is expanding over time as more tariffs are itemized.

caBaseChargeCallout.note: PG&E's Base Services Charge is income-graduated. We model the standard Tier 3 customer ($24.15/mo) for the headline figure. Lower-income customers pay less: Tier 1 (CARE/FERA) is $6.00/mo and Tier 2 is $12.08/mo. The range is stated so the headline is not read as cherry-picking the highest tier.

excludedStatesNote: States with only a fixed charge captured, a broken parse, or stale sources are not shown as itemized breakdowns: Wyoming, New Hampshire, Alaska, Idaho, and South Carolina. New Hampshire has no validated all-in rate and is excluded entirely; Alaska, Idaho, South Carolina, and Wyoming retain a single validated all-in rate but no breakdown.
